title: NOC 14202 – Collection Clerks | GTR Immigration
description: Collection Clerks play a key role in the financial ecosystem by managing overdue accounts, arranging repayment plans, and supporting credit-recovery operations across public and private institutions in Canada. Classified under NOC 14202, this occupation has experienced growing post-pandemic demand and is expected to remain in steady need—offering realistic immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Growth in consumer lending and credit servicesExpansion of regulatory and compliance activitiesEntry-level TEER occupation with advancement potentialEmployer-supported recruitmentIncreased focus on financial recovery operations
classification.major_group: 142 – Financial, insurance, and related administrative support workers
classification.minor_group: 1420 – Financial, insurance, and related administrative support workers

duties: Collection Clerks may perform some or all of the following:
Notify debtors of overdue payments by telephone or written correspondence
Arrange repayment plans and resolve collection issues
Recommend service suspension or further action when payments are not forthcoming
Trace and locate debtors through approved investigative methods
Prepare reports and maintain collection records
employment_requirements: Provincial or territorial collections licence (usually required)
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to generate approximately 3,900 new positions for Collection Clerks, while around 2,700 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Collection Clerks are in demand across Canada’s finance and credit-services sectors. Hiring has expanded since the pandemic and is projected to continue over the coming years.
Collection agenciesUtility companiesDepartment storesLoan companiesBanks and credit unionsGovernment financial and licensing departments
Annual salaries typically range between $26,700 and $61,600 CAD, depending on province, experience, and specialization.
